label: State Traversal (2D)
blurb: reading an input one symbol at a time and hopping a token between states along labeled arrows — the string is accepted only if the machine halts on an accepting state (finite state machines, regex engines, lexers/parsers, protocol & UI lifecycle states, turnstiles, traffic lights, vending logic).
classify: feeding an input symbol-by-symbol through a set of states wired together by labeled transition arrows, accepting iff the token halts on an accepting state (finite state machines / automata, regex matching, lexers/parsers, protocol or UI lifecycle states, turnstile/vending logic). Choose for 'state machine', 'automaton', 'transition', 'accept/reject a string' concepts.
levelFormat: node.level = {states:[string] (non-empty, unique ids), start:string (∈states), accepting:[string] (non-empty ⊆states), transitions:[{from:string∈states, on:string (non-empty symbol), to:string∈states}] (DETERMINISTIC — at most one arrow per from×on), input:[string] (non-empty symbols fed left→right)}. MUST be solvable: the deterministic run of `input` from `start` follows a defined arrow at every symbol and halts on an accepting state. Theme has vocab{state,symbol,machine,token,acceptWord,runWord,hint} + visual{accent,actorIcon}; each node entry {title,hook,winText,failText?} with failText keys "wrong-transition" and "incomplete".
drillLevelFormat: For state-traversal: node.level = {states,start,accepting,transitions:[{from,on,to}],input} — deterministic (≤1 arrow per state×symbol, all from/to ∈states) and solvable (the run of `input` from `start` has a defined arrow at each step and ends on an accepting state). failText keys are "wrong-transition" and "incomplete".
